title: I understand — or I don't
pedagogy: Vocabulary: เข้าใจ (high ข + mai tho → falling; mid จ + live ใ◌ → mid), รู้ (LOW ร + mai tho → high). Negation ไม่ + verb recombined into ไม่เข้าใจ and ไม่รู้ via sentence_build; register drill on particle choice (ค่ะ statements vs คะ questions, reviewing greet-01). Politeness flag: bare ไม่รู้ is curt with strangers (ไม่ทราบ mentioned as the polite counterpart, not taught).

exercises.title: The sentence that buys you time
body: เข้าใจ is literally “enter the heart” — when something is understood, it has gotten in.
Put yesterday's ไม่ in front and you own the most useful sentence in the language: ไม่เข้าใจ, “I don't understand”.
Said with a smile and a ครับ/ค่ะ, it turns confusion into a conversation instead of a standoff.

exercises.explanation: ไม่ + เข้าใจ — the same negation slot as ไม่ใช่. One pattern, endless mileage.
ไม่รู้ — “dunno”. Between friends it's honest; to a stranger, add ครับ/ค่ะ (the extra-polite version you'll hear back is ไม่ทราบ).

exercises.context: A taxi driver rattles off a question you didn't catch. You're a woman — tell him you don't understand.
exercises.question: What do you say?

choices.thai: ไม่เข้าใจค่ะ
ไม่เข้าใจคะ
ไม่เข้าใจครับ

exercises.explanation: You're stating a fact, so the female particle is falling-tone ค่ะ. High-tone คะ would turn it into a question, and ครับ is for male speakers.
